title: The Lighthouse Keeper
desc: The border beacons have gone dark. Time to relight them.

steps.text:
The signal beacons along the border are supposed to burn all night — a chain of light connecting the watchtowers from Cordac Falls to Stronghold Morn. But two of them went dark before midnight, and the garrison is too short-staffed to investigate.

A grizzled keeper presses a fire-flask into your hands. "Third beacon and fifth beacon. Third is probably just rain-soaked. Fifth..." He trails off. "Fifth goes out a lot. I don't like the fifth."

The trail between beacons is narrow, clinging to the cliff face. Wind howls up from the gorge below.

options.label: Head to the third beacon first
Go straight to the fifth beacon

steps.text:
The third beacon is a bronze brazier the size of a bathtub, perched on a stone platform overlooking the gorge. As predicted, the coals are waterlogged — the wind cover has been blown off its hinges.

You pour fire-flask fuel over the coals and strike a spark. The beacon roars back to life, throwing heat across the platform. You wire the wind cover back into place with a strip of leather from your belt.

From here you can see the fifth beacon's platform. It's dark. Completely dark. Not even embers.

You make the walk. When you get there, the brazier is cold. Stone cold. The coals aren't wet — they're gone. Taken. There are scuff marks on the stone where something dragged the coals away into the dark.

You refill the brazier from your flask, light it, and leave quickly. The keeper was right. You don't like the fifth either.

options.label: Report back — mention the scuff marks

steps.text:
The path to the fifth beacon is longer and steeper. By the time you reach the platform, you're winded and your fire-flask is sloshing against your hip.

The brazier is stone cold. No coals at all — not wet, not scattered, just gone. The platform is clean except for scuff marks that lead to the edge and then... stop. Whatever took the coals went over the side. Or up.

You refill the brazier, light it, and stand in its warmth for a long moment. From up here, you can see the third beacon — also dark, but that's just rain. You can fix that on the way back.

You do. The third beacon is a simple relight. The keeper nods when you return.

"The fifth?"
"Coals were gone again."
"Yeah." He doesn't ask anything else.
